structural_claim: Observable properties cannot possess predetermined values independent of measurement context, requiring fundamentally contextual descriptions

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Quantum observables cannot possess predetermined values independent of measurement context
Response to external perturbations and spontaneous fluctuations are universally related, making observables context-dependent on whether system is being measured or fluctuating freely
Equilibrium properties can be extracted from non-equilibrium processes, showing that thermodynamic observables depend on measurement protocol
